because.text: No P0 identity/settlement split. P1 cyclic-prior: record_roots recursed without a visited set -> two-record cycle crashed the verifier before per-record validation; fixed iteratively + shape-defensive in Python AND Go (the Go recordRoots had the identical memoize-after-recursion defect). P1 ski fail-open: verify_store swallowed RuntimeError silently (and a malformed-JSON check blob leaked JSONDecodeError past it - latent crash); run_ski_check now raises stable path-free reason classes, base verify WARNs `ski@v1 unverified: <class>`, settlement-grade escalates to ERR when the claim is settlement-active. P2-a malformed-JSON -> bounded `unloadable record` ERR, verification continues. P2-b WARRANT_REQUIRE_SIGMA strict mode fails the two silent SKIPs, CI runs strict. P2-c cryptography version-bounded, tests/hostile.py a required gate, per-Action SHA pin documented as follow-up (digests not guessed to avoid a red build). tests/hostile.py asserts no crash + Python/Go count agreement on cycles/self-cycle/deep-chain/cycle+valid/malformed-JSON/unverified-ski. All existing suites green; own store still 10 records 0/0. Verified locally on the MacBook.

subject.note: Codex v0.3 runtime hardening audit: no P0; two P1 (cyclic-prior crash, ski fail-open) + P2s fixed in both impls
